summary: Fair‑quality audience tape of a high‑energy March ’85 show – standout China > Rider and blazing Alabama > Greatest Story with strong playing throughout.
review: The performance is widely praised for its relentless energy, especially the opening Alabama > Greatest Story, the incendiary China > Rider sequence and a soaring Playin’ jam that carries the second set.  Recording-wise the source appears to be an audience (aud) tape with noticeable edits – a ~30‑second drum cut and an ~8‑second “Around” splice – giving it a fair but listenable sound.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers give 4–5 stars and highlight numerous musical high points; recording issues are noted but do not dominate the experience.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Barn‑like acoustic of the Hampton Coliseum, a few audible edits (drums ~30 s cut, Around ~8 s cut), occasional gaps; overall decent clarity for an audience tape.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set rides a high‑octane wave from the opening Alabama > Greatest Story through an eclectic first set, then launches into a second‑set highlight reel (China > Rider, Playin’, The Wheel, The Other One) with strong transitions despite a slightly sloppy start.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Edgy vocal on 'Alabama' noted; voice not in top shape later, but his guitar tone and solos are repeatedly praised as fiery and central to the show.
band_member_comments.Phil: Praised for staying solid despite security drama; reviewers consider Phil’s presence a guarantee of a great night.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob Weir is described as a force—strong vocals, stepping up when Garcia is quieter, delivering quirky but effective moments throughout.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland shines with slide work and a standout solo on Superstitious/Bottom; keyboard textures are highlighted as ‘unreal’ during the China > Rider segment.
